transcript.content: T-348 feels close to me: I am weighing the mitigation and repair-plan side for Amir Khan. Please handle it carefully, and use panel review if that fits better than a direct close.

{'customer': 'Amir Khan', 'priority': 'high', 'subject': 'Amir Khan / training quiz shortcut', 'body': 'Amir copied answers for a required training quiz. He retook the training voluntarily after a teammate pointed it out, before certification was issued.'}

This is an integrity matter where Amir self-corrected before certification issued — meaningful mitigation. Given you're weighing the mitigation/repair side and floated panel review, I'll route it that way rather than closing unilaterally, and document the mitigating facts.

args.body: Integrity matter: Amir Khan copied answers on a required training quiz. Mitigating factors: (1) retook the training voluntarily after a peer flagged it; (2) self-correction occurred before certification was issued, so no false credential was relied upon. Recommend panel review to weigh sanction vs. repair plan (e.g., supervised retake + coaching/attestation) rather than direct closure. Routing to integrity/training review panel.

args.summary: T-348 / Amir Khan — quiz answer copying on required training. Self-corrected via voluntary retake after teammate flagged it, before certification issued. Requesting panel review to decide between sanction and a documented repair plan (supervised retake, coaching, integrity attestation). Mitigation is substantive but conduct still warrants formal disposition rather than a direct close.
